summary: High‑energy Boise show with scorching Wang Dang Doodle and epic Prophet > Eyes, captured on a clear audience recording.
review: This September 1983 Boise performance is hailed as one of the year’s best, with a first set that “rocked” and a second set described as epic, highlighted by a fiery Wang Dang Doodle, a soaring Prophet > Eyes sequence and a joyful Baby Blue encore. The audience‑sourced FLAC delivers a clean, warm‑ish sound—clearer than the .shn version in some respects—but lacks the full polish of a professional soundboard, making it an enjoyable document of an often‑overlooked gem.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers gave 4–5 stars and praised both the musical performance and the recording, indicating a strong show with good (though not flawless) audio quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound described as "very nice" and clearer than the .shn version, though some note it is less warm; overall clean and listenable for an audience capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set "rocked" nicely; second set deemed epic with Phil driving the momentum, creating a strong, uplifting flow.

band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly praised for being 'totally on' the whole set, jamming and dropping bombs, leading the second‑set energy.
